nodes.body:
![medium](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/medium-priority.svg)

Use the `characterMap` for O(1) lookup of characters by ID.

```suggestion
                          .map(id => characterMap.get(id))
```

![medium](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/medium-priority.svg)

Import `calculateTotalLevel` from `@/lib/types` to reuse the existing utility function instead of re-implementing the level summation logic.

```suggestion
import { CharacterType, CharacterClass, calculateTotalLevel } from '@/lib/types';
```

![medium](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/medium-priority.svg)

Destructure `acNote` from the component props.

```suggestion
export function CharacterMiniSummary({
  name,
  race,
  characterType,
  classes,
  ac,
  acNote,
  hp,
  maxHp,
}: CharacterMiniSummaryProps) {
```

![medium](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/medium-priority.svg)

Use the `calculateTotalLevel` helper function to maintain consistency with other parts of the application.

```suggestion
  const totalLevel = classes ? calculateTotalLevel(classes) : 0;
```

![medium](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/medium-priority.svg)

Pass the `acNote` prop down to the `CombatStatsRow` component.

```suggestion
      <CombatStatsRow ac={ac} acNote={acNote} hp={hp} maxHp={maxHp} />
```

![medium](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/medium-priority.svg)

The `border-b` and `pb-4` styles are specific to the `CreatureStatBlock` layout and create unnecessary space and lines when used inside the `CharacterMiniSummary` cards. Consider removing these layout-specific styles from this primitive component and applying them in the consumer components instead.

```suggestion
    <div className="grid grid-cols-3 gap-4 text-sm">
```

![medium](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/medium-priority.svg) Wrap the `CombatStatsRow` in a container that provides the necessary border and padding to maintain the original visual design of the stat block. ```suggestion
```

`partyCharacters` silently drops unknown/missing `characterIds` (filtered out after `find`). This can make the displayed member list disagree with `Members: {party.characterIds.length}` and removes the previous visible "Unknown" placeholder. Consider either rendering an explicit placeholder for missing IDs or basing the member count/list off the same resolved set so the UI stays consistent.

This change directory is missing the `.openspec.yaml` manifest that other OpenSpec changes include (typically generated by `openspec new change`). Add the manifest (and any required scaffolding) so OpenSpec tooling can recognize and manage this change consistently.

This tasks doc references `npm test` / `npm test -- ...` and `npm run typecheck`, but `package.json` doesn't define `test` or `typecheck` scripts. Update these commands to the repo's actual scripts (e.g., `npm run test:unit`, `npm run test:integration`, and `npx tsc --noEmit`) so the instructions are runnable.

`renderComponent` appends a container and creates a React root but never unmounts or removes the container, which can leak DOM between tests. Align with existing test helpers (e.g., `createReactRoot`/`unmountReactRoot` or `setupUiTest`) or add an `afterEach` cleanup.

Same as above: this test helper creates a root and appends a container without unmounting/removing it, which can cause cross-test leakage. Prefer the repo's `createReactRoot`/`unmountReactRoot` helpers or add explicit cleanup in `afterEach`.

`totalLevel` duplicates the existing `calculateTotalLevel` helper in `lib/types.ts`. Using the shared helper (handling the optional/empty case) would reduce duplication and keep level calculations consistent across the app.

<sub>:red_circle: HIGH RISK</sub>

The refactored CreatureStatBlock is currently uncovered by tests (0% diff coverage). Since this component replaces logic that was previously covered, please update the unit tests to verify the component correctly integrates with the new shared CombatStatsRow and ensures no visual regressions occurred regarding AC, AC notes, and HP display.
<!-- e34d5167-b092-49eb-b8c8-33859ab00079 -->

<sub>:yellow_circle: MEDIUM RISK</sub>

Suggestion: The logic for grouping and rendering party members is currently O(P*M*C) due to nested .find() lookups within maps and is structured as a complex IIFE inside JSX. Consider extracting this logic into a dedicated 'PartyMemberGrid' component. Additionally, create a characterMap using useMemo to index characters by ID, enabling O(1) lookups and improving overall dashboard performance.
<!-- e34d5167-b092-49eb-b8c8-33859ab00079 -->

<sub>:white_circle: LOW RISK</sub>

Suggestion: The CombatStatsRow component contains hardcoded layout styles (border-b and pb-4). While these work for the full Stat Block, they create unnecessary trailing borders and padding when used as the final element in CharacterMiniSummary cards. Update the component to accept a 'showBorder' prop or custom className to toggle these styles.
<!-- e34d5167-b092-49eb-b8c8-33859ab00079 -->

`partyCharacters` is built by mapping IDs to `characterMap.get(id)` and then filtering out missing characters. This changes behavior vs the prior implementation (which showed `Unknown` for missing IDs) by silently dropping missing party members and reordering could diverge from `party.characterIds`. Consider preserving the original `characterIds` order and rendering an explicit placeholder for missing characters (or at least surfacing a "missing member" indicator) so parties with stale IDs are not silently altered.

The displayed member count now uses `partyCharacters.length` (only characters found in the fetched list), which can undercount compared to `party.characterIds.length` and hide data inconsistencies. If the intent is to show party membership, keep the count based on `party.characterIds.length` (and optionally show how many are missing from the character lookup).

The spec calls out "No additional fetches on render" for the grouped member UI, but the test suite here doesn't assert the number of `fetch` calls. Add an assertion (after effects have settled) that only the expected initial requests were made and that rendering grouped members doesn't trigger extra network calls.

The OpenSpec change spec includes an NPC-only scenario (party with NPCs and no PCs hides the PC section), but this test file doesn't cover it. Add a test where the party contains only `characterType: 'npc'` members and assert the Player Characters and Companions sections are not rendered.

The requirements specify each member card shows name, race, class, level, AC, and HP. Current assertions only check member names (and absence of comma list). Add assertions that at least one rendered `CharacterMiniSummary` displays race, class, level, and the AC/HP fraction to ensure the card content meets the spec.

OpenSpec requires that `CharacterMiniSummary` makes no network requests on mount, but the unit tests don't currently assert this. Add a test that spies on `global.fetch` (or ensures it's undefined) and verifies it is not called during render.

This test name says "renders ability scores in compact mode" but it renders with `isCompact: false` (non-compact) and asserts ability scores are present. Rename the test to reflect the non-compact/full mode to avoid confusion when diagnosing failures.

The class examples here use `{name:"Fighter", level: ...}` but the actual `CharacterClass` type in this repo uses the `class` property (e.g. `{ class: "Fighter", level: 5 }`). Update these examples so the spec/tests documentation matches the real data model.

The `classes` examples use `{name:"Fighter", level: ...}` but the codebase's `CharacterClass` interface uses `class` (see `lib/types.ts`). Updating the examples to `{ class: "Fighter", level: ... }` will keep the spec aligned with the actual API/type shape.

This multiclass example also uses `{name:"Fighter"...}`/`{name:"Rogue"...}` but the repo's `CharacterClass` type uses `class`. Update the example objects to avoid misleading implementers/readers of the spec.
